Queensland Racing Integrity Commission deputy commissioner Kim Kelly has put in his resignation, alleging that forthcoming organisational changes announced in last year's The Next Lap report will remove the QRIC's ability to be independent of Racing Queensland.

Home Affairs moved to within $5000 of Snitzel in the Champion 2YO Sires’ Premiership after juvenile winners in New South Wales and Victoria, while King Pedro stormed home to win the Listed Grafton Cup and hand New Zealand-based sire Eminent his first stakes winner.
With a fortnight to the end of the 2025/26 racing season, the Champion Sire premiership is clinched for Snitzel, but for some of the premierships, every meeting and every win is going to count.

Toronado was the only stallion to sire two individual stakes winners this Hong Kong season, while Deep Field topped the sire table by winners. Acclamation might have won Champion Sire by earnings, but there are several new kids on the block who look like they might challenge next season.

Racing Victoria have released its spring feature program. Shaped by the Moonee Valley rebuild project and the MRC's 150th anniversary, the program includes a $6 million purse for the G1 Caulfield Cup and a third golden ticket race for the G1 Melbourne Cup at Flemington.

Band Of Brothers showcased his toughness to claim Listed Ramornie Handicap glory at Grafton, while promising juveniles American Eagle, Castillo King, and Oh Yes She Did all enhanced their reputations with impressive metropolitan maiden victories.

TABcorp and Racing NSW have announced an agreement to approve the introduction of a National Tote, bound by the firm belief that the move will future-proof racing. But what does the National Tote actually promise, and how will the two entities deliver this vision?

Seven racefillies and broodmares attracted winning bids of $100,000 or more in this week's Inglis Digital sale, led by Mogumber Park's blue chip mare Mindarie, who sold for $355,000 to Robert Bavcevic. Young mares Pivotal Ten and Summer Success each sold for $190,000.
